methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $38M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Swan is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company founded by Dima Kislovskiy that has raised $38.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 private and 2 public companies operating in the same sector at similar or adjacent stages, including peers such as Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $38.5M total raised as a proxy for current value and applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for advancement from Seed to the next stage, implying a valuation of $96M. Peer-median analysis examines four directly comparable companies—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—yielding a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M.

Synthesizing these two methods produces a triangulated valuation range of $82M (low), $161M (median), to $259M (high). The stage-progression method anchors to Swan's actual capital deployment, while peer-median reflects market pricing for comparable risk profiles in the Defense & Security sector. The $177M spread between low and high estimates reflects meaningful dispersion in peer valuations and inherent uncertainty around Swan's specific competitive positioning, unit economics, and revenue trajectory relative to the comp set.
